contents:
# TeamPCP cybercrime group launches wiper malware campaign against Iran

## Overview
A new cybercrime group called TeamPCP executed a wiper malware attack on Iranian targets over the weekend. The campaign destroyed data on compromised systems. It followed the group's prior activities targeting cloud environments.

## Details
The attack materialized this past weekend, with experts attributing it to TeamPCP, known since December 2025 for exploiting Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, Redis servers, and React2Shell vulnerabilities. TeamPCP used a self-propagating worm to compromise corporate cloud setups, then moved laterally to steal credentials and extort via Telegram. No specific casualty figures reported, but it disrupted victim networks. The group remains relatively new and focuses on data destruction in this instance.[2]

**Location**: Iran

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @AllSource4, which covers all-source intelligence, multi-domain OSINT, military movements. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following all-source intelligence will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
